corrections.description: Missing the beginning of the Hobby Feature article
corrections.text: Summer picnics can be great fun, whether you and your family take to the canyon, the woods, or the beach, or whether you prefer just to relax and enjoy a cookout in your own back yard. What could be more satisfying on a warm summer day than a delicious thirst-quenching cold drink or a slice of cooled melon? But how to keep the melons and the drinks cold is the question. For this practical purpose, and for aesthetic appeal, as well, the washtub in the picture was painted and decorated. Such colorful tubs, when not in use for keeping foods cold, can serve, also, as containers for special arrangements of flowers and greenery for enhancing the attractiveness of the outdoor rest and recreation area. The project of decorating washtubs proved to be one of the most exciting and rewarding homemaking projects sponsored by the Relief Society sisters of the Parley's Sixth Ward, Salt Lake City, Utah. Even those with no acknowledged creativity found real satisfaction in wielding a paint brush with such delightful results. Originality was the key word. White, black or red paint was used to cover the tubs, with a variety of colors and designs used for trim. Sisters with some artistic talent painted original, free-hand designs, such as the one shown in the picture. The more timid ones used decals, stencils, or tracings.

corrections.description: Contains recipe content that belongs in different article
corrections.text: Fill the decorated tub with favorite drinks, fruit juice, milk, homemade rootbeer, lemonade, or melons. Pack with ice, and a large part of the picnic preparations have been completed.

corrections.description: Contains Relief Society Activities content that should be separate
corrections.text: A teaching aid packet is available for the cultural refinement class leaders. It will contain four full color reproductions of famous art masterpieces (printed in the June and September 1969 color sections of the Magazines) to be used with cultural refinement lessons 2, 3, 5, and 6, as designated. It will also contain a 12'' 33% rpm record of the musical selections to be used with cultural refinement lessons, 1, 4, and 7. The kit is available from the Deseret Book Company, 44 East South Temple, Salt Lake City, Utah 84110, or at the Deseret Book Company branches in Ogden, Utah the Cottonwood Mall in Salt Lake City, Utah, or Orange California, at a cost of $3.50 plus 30 cents postage for third class mailing. Overseas should be sent by international money order, plus air mail charges, approximate weight 1 Ib. (See advertisement page 637.)

notes: Major extraction issues found. Many files contain wrong content entirely, suggesting systematic problems with article boundary detection. Multiple lesson department articles are mixed up or contain wrong content. Several files have content bleeding from adjacent articles. The extraction appears to have significant problems with article boundaries and content assignment.
